summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. JWT auth (register/login/logout/me/refresh), brute-force lockout, cart/wishlist persistence, share validation hardening, and catalog regressions all pass end-to-end. Backend: 36/36 tests pass (16 new auth+share + 20 iteration-2 regression). Frontend: login/signup/logout, profile menu, cart round-trip across fresh browser context, home page (no reviews, no hero stats) all verified.

minor.issue: server.py sets allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=CORS_ORIGINS which is '*' from backend/.env. Browsers reject wildcard-with-credentials responses; works today only because the frontend is same-origin behind the ingress. Consider replacing with an explicit origin list for production hardening.
response.set_cookie call hardcodes secure=False and does not reuse set_auth_cookies helper (minor DRY violation).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py CORS: allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*'] is spec-incompatible. Replace CORS_ORIGINS default with an explicit origin list before production launch.
routes/auth.py refresh() duplicates the cookie-setting logic instead of calling set_auth_cookies (minor DRY issue).

The admin cart in Mongo was polluted from earlier iterations (contained a b5 item with only {id, qty}). Not a bug in this iteration's code, but the frontend cart drawer renders NaN when an item lacks a price field. Consider hardening the cart UI to guard against missing fields (item.price || 0) and/or filtering incomplete cart items on GET /api/auth/state.

AuthContext initial user state is null while checking and false when logged out — pages that render Login button before /me resolves may briefly show a signed-out state; consider a loading skeleton for the profile-btn.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@sagadrop.com / SagaAdmin@2026. Test users are dynamically registered with TEST_<uuid>@example.com prefix.
seed_data_creation: During the test run, ~4-5 TEST_<uuid>@example.com users were registered in db.users and 1-2 login_attempts rows created. All test data uses TEST_ prefix for identification/cleanup. Also, one TEST_okpng share doc was created in db.shared_books.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests are split across two files: backend_test.py (iteration-2 regression) and test_auth_and_share.py (iteration-3 auth+share+catalog). Both are pytest-compatible and run under `pytest /app/backend/tests/`. Auth uses httpOnly cookies (access_token 15min, refresh_token 7d). The test file has a pytest.ini in /app/backend that auto-enables xdist LoadScope — so tests already run in parallel. Brute-force lockout is 5 fails / 15 min per (ip:email). To avoid locking out the admin, always use throwaway TEST_ emails for lockout tests. Admin cart in Mongo may accumulate stale items across test runs; last test reset it to empty via /api/auth/state PUT.
verified_flows: POST /api/auth/register creates user, sets httpOnly cookies, returns public user; duplicate email → 409
GET /api/auth/me with cookie returns admin; without cookie → 401
POST /api/auth/logout clears cookies; /me on new session → 401
POST /api/auth/refresh rotates access token using refresh cookie
POST /api/share rejects image/gif (400), invalid base64 (400); accepts valid PNG
GET /api/books?limit=40 returns 40 books, 5 per each of 8 categories
All 40 cover URLs on covers.openlibrary.org respond 200 with real (non-default) images (>500 bytes)
Frontend: /login sign-in with admin → home, profile-btn 'A', profile-menu with logout works
Frontend: /signup creates unique user & logs in; duplicate email shows auth-error
Frontend: go-to-login-link and go-to-signup-link navigate correctly
Frontend: add to cart → debounced save → cart restored on fresh browser context after login
Frontend: home page no longer has 'What Readers Whisper' reviews section, no hero stats (50K+ Books etc.)
Frontend: hero 3D canvas renders, trending shows 6 book cards with real covers, categories shows 8 tiles
